title: Tristaniopsis merguensis
taxonomy: Tristaniopsis merguensis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It belongs to the genus Tristaniopsis, which is part of the larger family of myrtles. Within the Myrtaceae family, Tristaniopsis merguensis is also classified as being in the order Myrtales. The species is further classified as being in the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Tristaniopsis merguensis, also known as the Indian coral tree, is a deciduous tree species native to the tropical regions of India, Southeast Asia, and the Pacific Islands. The health benefits of Tristaniopsis merguensis include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various diseases such as fever, cough, and skin infections.
